news.headline: Denison Mines Corp reports strong financial results for 2024, with progress on the Phoenix Project.
news.summary: On March 13, 2025, Denison Mines Corp. (TSX: DML, NYSE American: DNN) submitted its Audited Consolidated Financial Statements and Management's Discussion & Analysis for the fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2024. The company provided a comprehensive financial overview for stakeholders to review.

news.headline: Hayashi: US, Japan cost-sharing on stationed troops suitable
Japan Chief Cabinet Secretary Yoshimasa Hayashi speaks in Tokyo
ADENTRA's 2024 results showed $2.18B in sales, with $530.8M in Q4 sales, $0.34 EPS, $42.2M EBITDA, and a 21.7% gross margin.
news.summary: ADENTRA Inc., a distributor of architectural building products in North America, reported financial results for Q4 2024, with sales of US$530.8 million, a 3.1% increase from Q4 2023. Full-year sales were $2.18 billion, a 2.5% decrease from 2023. The company's gross margin increased to 21.7% in 2024, with operating expenses rising to $377.2 million. Earnings per share were $1.95 in 2024, while adjusted earnings per share were $3.01. Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 was $184.3 million, with Q4 2024 seeing $42.2 million. The company generated $142.8 million in cash flow in 2024, acquired Woolf Distributing Company for $130 million, and raised dividends by 7%. Despite market headwinds in 2025, ADENTRA is positioned for stability due to its market presence, with limited tariff exposure. The company will hold an investor call on March 14, 2025, to discuss the results.

news.headline: Georgia legislature approves SB 144; Alliance commends move to protect farmers' access to crop protection tools.
news.summary: Today, Georgia lawmakers showed overwhelming bipartisan support by passing Senate Bill 144, a crucial legislation aimed at securing farmers' access to essential crop protection tools. This bold move sends a strong signal to states nationwide to take action. The bill's approval underscores the importance of supporting agriculture, with a focus on ensuring the continued ability to feed America.

news.headline: Senate Democrats considering options as government shutdown nears due to disagreement over budget.
Samsung SDI plans to raise $1.4 billion through new share sales for battery plant expansion.
ECB President Christine Lagarde: Speaks in BBC Interview
ECB President Lagarde: Donald Trump decisions cause concern BBC
ECB President Lagarde: trade conflict will damage worldwide economy
UK provides £2 billion in loans to defense companies for supporting their allies, promoting national security.
Stocks fell with Trump citing "globalists." Nvidia, Tesla, Palantir, Netflix declined. Broadcom rose. Jobs report awaited.
news.summary: Stocks plummeted today following President Trump's criticism of "globalists." Leading tech companies like Nvidia, Tesla, Palantir, and Netflix experienced significant losses, while Broadcom saw a late surge. The Dow Jones was particularly impacted. Investors are now awaiting the upcoming jobs report, which is anticipated to provide further insights into the market's trajectory. The developments caused major fluctuations in the stock market, alerting traders and analysts to closely monitor the situation.

news.headline: Dow Jones falls on surprise jobs report, Nvidia partner crashes on earnings. Fed Chair Powell's speech upcoming.
news.summary: On Friday, the Dow Jones experienced a decline in response to an unexpected jobs report, causing concern among investors. The upcoming speech by Federal Reserve Chair Powell is awaited anxiously, as it may impact the market further. In a significant development, tech giant Nvidia's partner, Hewlett Packard, suffered a notable crash following its earnings report. This news highlights the volatility in the market as numbers of major indices fluctuate based on various economic indicators and corporate performances.

news.headline: Stock market sees major indexes down, Nvidia and Tesla up after previous declines. Volatile day of trading.
news.summary: On Tuesday, the stock market saw major indexes declining with Nvidia and Tesla managing to recover slightly following significant drops the day before. The volatile session concluded with indexes ending lower in a whipsaw action. Although the overall trend was negative for the major indexes, Nvidia and Tesla were able to finish in a positive territory. The live coverage of the stock market today highlighted this fluctuation in prices.

news.headline: LyondellBasell CEO to speak at J.P. Morgan 2025 Industrials Conference in NYC on March 12, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. EDT.
news.summary: In Houston and London, it was reported on March 05, 2025, that LyondellBasell revealed its CEO, Peter Vanacker, is set to engage in a fireside discussion at the J.P. Morgan 2025 Industrials Conference in New York City. The event is scheduled for Wednesday, March 12, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. EDT.

news.headline: Silexion Therapeutics reports positive data on SIL204 reducing tumors and metastases in pancreatic cancer models.
news.summary: Recent data from Silexion Therapeutics shows that when subcutaneously administered, SIL204 has been successful in reducing both primary tumors and metastases in clinically significant orthotopic models. These models replicate the environment where human pancreatic cancer cells naturally grow. The findings indicate a promising advancement in cancer treatment, with SIL204 showing potential in combating the spread of cancer cells in their native environment. This research could be a significant step forward in addressing pancreatic cancer, impacting a large number of individuals affected by this disease.

news.headline: Fed's Bostic cautions on rate cuts as economic shifts cause uncertainty. Rates remain at 4.25%-4.50%. Clarification likely by late spring or summer.
news.summary: Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic stated that Federal Reserve officials may have to wait until the summer for enough clarity to adjust interest rates due to significant economic uncertainty caused by policy changes under the Trump administration. Bostic mentioned at an event hosted by the Birmingham Business Journal that amidst the ongoing transitions, it is hard to predict the outcomes. He expressed doubt that clarity would be achieved before late spring or summer, emphasizing the need for patience. The Fed had reduced rates by a full percentage point in 2024 and currently maintains the benchmark rate in the 4.25%-4.50% range. It is anticipated that policymakers will maintain this rate for the time being.

news.headline: First Quantum finalizes $1 billion senior notes offering.
February jobs report shows U.S. economy added 151,000 jobs (below 160,000 expected) with higher than expected unemployment rate of 4.1% (above 4.0%). Labor market still concerning.
news.summary: The latest jobs report for February revealed that the U.S. economy added 151,000 jobs, falling short of the anticipated 160,000. The unemployment rate also saw an unexpected increase to 4.1%, surpassing the expected 4.0%. This data, monitored by Trading Economics, has prompted various experts to comment on the situation. Liz Young Thomas, the head of investment strategy at SoFi, expressed her unease regarding the labor market, citing the rise in both unemployment and underemployment, which increased from 7.5% to 8%. Despite the unexpected numbers, the U.S. services sector saw growth in February, even amidst slowing job creation. The comprehensive story is available for further reading.

news.headline: Beyond Oil gets global approval from Restaurant Brands International for 30,000+ locations, paving the way for significant global growth.
news.summary: One of the world's largest quick-service companies has granted global brand approval to Beyond Oil, marking a significant milestone in their global expansion. This approval opens up vast growth opportunities within the quick-service restaurant industry for Beyond Oil.

news.headline: Fed Chair Powell emphasizes patience amidst market uncertainty & trade turmoil. No rush to adjust interest rates.
news.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is proceeding cautiously, examining the effects of President Donald Trump's changing trade policies on economic stability. He is refraining from making immediate changes to monetary policy. Powell emphasized the U.S. economy's stability while addressing a University of Chicago event on Friday. He stated that there is no pressing need to modify interest rates at the moment until the economic situation becomes clearer. This decision comes amidst increasing economic uncertainty stemming from evolving trade policies. Powell's remarks indicate a deliberate and strategic approach to managing the economy in the face of market fluctuations, as detailed in an article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: February jobs report had modest hiring, amid DOGE job cuts; Fed Chair Powell sees solid labor market. S&P 500 rebounds.
news.summary: In February, the jobs report revealed slight employment growth, coinciding with the beginning of layoffs in the Department of General Employment (DOGE) and the implementation of tariffs initiated by former President Trump by the middle of the month. Despite the softer labor market indicated by the report, Federal Reserve Chair Powell described it as 'solid'. Additionally, the S&P 500 index experienced a rebound during this time period.

news.headline: US auto sector faces earnings risk due to tariff uncertainty, with potential production cuts and rising costs. Tariffs on USMCA goods delayed again.
news.summary: The escalating trade tensions between the U.S. and its key trading partners are causing concerns for the automotive industry. Goldman Sachs analyst Mark Delaney warns that tariffs pose a significant earnings risk for U.S. automakers and industrial tech companies, with potential reductions in vehicle production and rising costs for imported parts. Delaney suggests that uncertainty around tariff policy is leading companies to accelerate imports in early 2025 to build inventory, but long-term operational changes are unlikely until the situation stabilizes. Tariffs on USMCA-compliant goods were initially set to take effect on March 4 but were delayed on March 7 by an executive order from President Trump. This is the second delay in tariff enforcement.

news.headline: Vaccinex plans to delist its Common Stock from Nasdaq after suspension since Dec 2024. Company uses SEMA4D blockage to treat neurodegenerative diseases.
news.summary: On March 07, 2025, Vaccinex, Inc. (OTC: VCNX) revealed its choice to delist the Company's common stock from the Nasdaq Stock Market. The clinical-stage biotechnology company has been focused on a novel approach to treating neurodegenerative diseases by targeting astrogliosis and neuroinflammation via SEMA4D inhibition. Nasdaq halted trading of Vaccinex's common stock on December 18, 2024.

news.headline: CIBC Innovation Banking gives growth capital financing to SpryPoint, leading software provider for utilities.
news.summary: CIBC Innovation Banking in Toronto has recently extended a growth-focused credit facility to SpryPoint Services Inc., a prominent software provider for public utilities. Through a cloud-native platform, SpryPoint aids utilities in enhancing customer service and operational efficiency. Their innovative meter-to-cash lifecycle approach enables utilities to evolve from basic functions to playing more integral roles in the modernization process, thereby delivering significant and lasting value for their customers.

news.headline: NextNRG, Inc. completes public offering, raising $50 million.
LAVA considering strategic choices.
Democrats are seeking clarity on the fate of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau as Jonathan McKernan faces Senate for confirmation.
news.summary: When Jonathan McKernan appears before the Senate Banking Committee on Thursday for his confirmation hearing to lead the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, the primary focus may not be on him but rather on the future of the CFPB.

news.headline: February job report shows steady growth with 151k jobs added, but concerns rise over federal cutbacks and tariffs impact.
news.summary: In February, a total of 151,000 jobs were added by employers, as reported by the Labor Department. This increase occurred during the period when the policies of the Trump administration were still being implemented.

news.headline: Teleflex to split into 2 companies: Urology, Acute Care, and OEM into new entity. Shareholders to receive tax-free shares.
news.summary: In Wayne, Pennsylvania on February 27, 2025, Teleflex Incorporated, a prominent medical technologies provider listed on NYSE under the symbol TFX, revealed plans to split its Urology, Acute Care, and OEM divisions into a separate publicly traded entity. The decision came after a thorough assessment of its business portfolio. The company's Board of Directors granted permission to Teleflex management to proceed with this strategic move, which involves distributing newly issued shares of the new company, NewCo, to existing shareholders. This distribution is structured to be tax-free for U.S. tax purposes.

news.headline: Executives face turmoil as Trump's trade policies cause chaos, with investors feeling the impact of the shifting trade policy.
news.summary: Business leaders are concerned about the uncertainty caused by President Trump's trade policy, leading to chaos in boardrooms. Investors are also feeling the impact, with stock prices declining as a result of the shifting trade policies. The unpredictability has unsettled markets, with the S&P 500 falling by 2.5% and the Dow Jones dropping by 3% in response to the news. The trade policy changes have created a sense of instability, causing unease among corporate executives and investors alike.

news.headline: Fed Chair Powell reassures no rush to adjust rates amidst Trump policy uncertainty. Prioritizing clarity amidst tariff changes.
news.summary: Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve Chair, has emphasized the importance of distinguishing "signal from noise" amidst the ongoing fluctuations in tariffs under the current presidency. Powell emphasized the need for careful analysis and decision-making to navigate through the unpredictable fluctuations.

news.headline: Analyst upgrades Viavi Solutions to Buy, sets price target at $14. Expects sustained growth in telco and optical markets, with NE segment sales rising 15% YoY and 26% quarterly.
news.summary: Viavi Solutions (NASDAQ: VIAV) was upgraded by Rosenblatt analyst Mike Genovese from Neutral to Buy, with a price forecast of $14. The bullish stance is due to the impressive performance of the Network Enablement (NE) instruments business, which saw a 15% year-over-year and 26% sequential sales increase in the December quarter. Genovese believes this growth is sustainable and expects strong momentum to continue in the upcoming quarters. The company also raised its guidance for total revenues and NE segment revenue for the third quarter, surpassing seasonal trends. This upgrade signals positive prospects for Viavi Solutions moving forward.

news.headline: Clothing retailer Forever 21 considers liquidation amid bankruptcy, possibly within days.
Shein stays committed to IPO amid US tariff challenges, aims to raise over $1 billion.
Trump responded to market backlash by delaying tariffs on Mexico and Canada. Inflation concerns as prices remain elevated.
news.summary: The Trump administration is taking caution due to high prices, as there is a concern about potentially exacerbating inflation with import tariffs.

news.headline: Debate over Trump's tax cuts: Could cost trillions or nothing to avoid tax increases.
news.summary: Is the government spending trillions of dollars or nothing to prevent tax increases? This question is straightforward.

news.headline: Costamare Inc. plans to spin-off its dry bulk business into Costamare Bulkers Holdings Limited creating two distinct public companies.
news.summary: On February 27, 2025, in Monaco, Costamare Inc. (NYSE: CMRE) revealed its plan to separate its dry bulk business to form a new entity named Costamare Bulkers Holdings Limited. This strategic move will lead to the establishment of two distinct publicly traded firms.

news.headline: Trump focused on his vision for America in his first speech to Congress since presidency. Little mention of Republicans.
news.summary: During his recent address to a joint session of Congress, Donald Trump, who has returned to the presidency, notably avoided discussing his fellow Republicans, a notable absence in his speech.

news.headline: HCI Group, Inc. (NYSE: HCI) restructures into two units for long-term alignment, introducing Exzeo Group Inc.
news.summary: HCI Group, Inc. (NYSE: HCI) revealed a new organizational setup in Tampa, Fla. on March 3, 2025. The company will now operate through two separate units, each with clear management teams and objectives. This change aims to enhance HCI's future growth and potential.

news.headline: Many undocumented workers are staying home due to fear of deportation. Labor shortages are worsening in construction, agriculture, senior care, and hospitality sectors.
news.summary: With the threat of increased immigration enforcement, numerous immigrants are opting to remain indoors, leading to labor shortages in various sectors. Industries such as construction, agriculture, senior care, and hospitality are already grappling with a lack of workers, and this situation is expected to exacerbate further. The overall workforce deficiency is anticipated to intensify as more immigrants choose to avoid potential roundups, impacting crucial sectors that rely heavily on immigrant labor.

news.headline: Since Trump's election win, Palantir Technologies' stock (NASDAQ:PLTR) surged the most, up 59.91% from $51.13 to $81.76.
news.summary: Since President Donald Trump's election on Nov. 5, 2024, and subsequent inauguration, financial markets have experienced volatility. Six companies in the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE:SPY) have seen a surge, with returns exceeding 30% since that date. The leading performer is Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:PLTR), skyrocketing by 59.91% from $51.13 to $81.76. Based in Denver, Colorado, the company has achieved an annualized return of over 184%. This surge is attributed to its capitalization on AI trends and CEO Alex Karp's backing of Elon Musk's governmental efficiency initiatives. Karp defends these efforts as crucial in combating fraud, waste, and abuse. Benzinga Pro's data indicates Palantir's momentum and potential for continued growth.

news.headline: DOGE jobs report shows Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency reducing government contracts and workforces at federal agencies.
news.summary: Elon Musk's Government Efficiency Department is significantly reducing government contracts and workforces at sensitive federal agencies.

news.headline: Democracy Forward submits requests to federal agencies seeking info on AI use in personnel decisions.
news.summary: Today, Democracy Forward is filing public records requests with numerous federal agencies to obtain details about the implementation of artificial intelligence in the decision-making process regarding personnel. The requests aim to uncover how AI is being utilized in making personnel choices within these agencies.

news.headline: Trump does not rule out a potential recession in 2025, citing uncertainty; refuses to predict.
news.summary: Trump refused to speculate on the likelihood of a recession when asked during a recent interview. The discussion took place on "Sunday Morning Futures with Maria Bartiromo."

news.headline: Trump may reduce or cancel 25% tariffs on Mexico and Canada, officials suggest.
news.summary: Officials from the Trump administration are indicating that the president may reduce or eliminate the proposed 25 percent tariffs on Mexico and Canada.

news.headline: Yen weakens 0.2% to 148.10 per dollar
VAALCO Energy, Inc. reports Q4 & full year 2024 results, record reserves. Provides 2025 guidance.
news.summary: Houston-based energy company, VAALCO Energy, Inc. (NYSE: EGY, LSE: EGY), shared its performance for Q4 and full year of 2024 along with its year-end 2024 reserves. The company outlined its operational and financial results and set expectations for Q1 and full year of 2025. VAALCO revealed its plans for the year ahead, indicating the strategic direction and key focus areas. This update marks a significant step for the company as it navigates the energy sector landscape in the coming quarters.

news.headline: Ethereum delays Pectra upgrade, sets up Hoodi testnet. Pectra may go live on mainnet by April 25 with over 10 EIPs.
news.summary: Ethereum core developers are introducing a third testnet named Hoodi to assess the Pectra upgrade after initial bugs. Pectra's mainnet launch is delayed to late April, to be activated on Hoodi on March 26. If successful, Pectra could go live on Ethereum mainnet by April 25, enhancing wallets and user experience with 10 Improvement Proposals. The upgrade aims to double the blob count for data availability and was initially set for a late 2024 launch. Galaxy Digital plans to mimic the Ethereum mainnet's operation on Hoodi, distributing at least 20 million test staked-Ether across 11 client teams and five staking operators. The third testnet is part of Ethereum's quicker upgrade deployment strategy to stay competitive in the blockchain sector.

news.headline: S. Korea Choi Financial, Forex Markets Face External Uncertainties
